title: LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS - 211info
text: CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people to housing programs, including shelter information, other housing resources, tenant support, and non-housing resources. Does not provide emergency housing or rental assistance, and there may be a waitlist. 1025 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Must be a current participant of OASIS drop-in center Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-4pm, Wednesday 8am-6pm, closed holidays Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 345-3628 Main phone Main phone (541) 345-3628 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Must be referred. Call for information. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Lane County SITE HOURS: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-4pm, Thursday 9am-4pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located at the corner of 10th and G Streets. On LTD Bus Line 18. Services: Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Homeless Families Other Services Offered At This Location: FOOD - FOOD PANTRY HOUSING/SHELTER - HOMELESSNESS PREVENTION HOUSING/SHELTER - OASIS DROP-IN DAY CENTER INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - FAMILY SUPPORT AND CONNECTIONS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - REFUGEE AND IMMIGRANT SERVICES PROGRAM MATERIAL GOODS - CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES COMMUNITY SERVICE CENTER UTILITIES - UTILITY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD
